MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-30-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David B. Simpson, Esq., Wolflick & Simpson, 818-243-8300, for Defendant and moving party: Pacific Directory (sued as Southwestern Bell)
Dow. W. Patten, Esq., Law Offices of Spencer Smith, 415-520-0104, for Plaintiff and opposing party: Sonya Bryant
Argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL DIRECTORY (SUED AS SOUTHWESTERN BELL)'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Motion for Summary judgment Denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted as to punitive damages claims. Plaintiffs present no evidence that any officer, director, or managing agent of defendant either engaged in, authorized, or ratified any allegedly wrongful conduct. CC 3294(b). Summary adjudication denied in all other respects. Triable issues of fact raised regarding discrimination and pretext: Plintiffs' non-African American male counterparts failed to reach the numbers of the performance plan, yet were not placed on PIPS and did not have key accounts removed, but received key accounts taken from plaintiffs; those counterparts performances were cured by the ad-hoc application of mitigators. However, when plaintiffs requested mitigators they were denied. The performance plan was a sham because no writing setting it forth was given to plaintiffs by their supervisors, and the performance plan was never "rolled out."
Triable issues of fact raised regarding retaliation: Complaints were filed by plaintiffs both internally and externally including the early EEO complaint against Sample and Nantria, informal complaints of discrimination in 12/08. EEO complaints about Nett, complaints of discrimination and retaliation with CA Department of Fair Employment and Housing and right to sue letters of 10/2/09. Retaliation raised in suspervision of Pete without pay in 2/09, not getting back key accounts after being taken off the PIP, not having mitigators applied, and remaining on PIPs. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153
